content.text:
Here are some of the **top AI headlines as of Thursday, April 9, 2026** that stood out from recent coverage:

- **OpenAI outlines its preferred AI economic policy agenda** — TechCrunch reports OpenAI is publicly pushing ideas such as public wealth funds, robot taxes, and even a four-day workweek as part of its broader vision for how AI may reshape labor and the economy. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/06/openais-vision-for-the-ai-economy-public-wealth-funds-robot-taxes-and-a-four-day-work-week/?utm_source=openai))
- **The White House calls for a lighter-touch federal AI framework** — AP reports the administration urged Congress to preempt stricter state AI laws and adopt a national approach focused on innovation, child safety, IP, electricity costs, and education. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/479eb3d0a50fe7237678a9bfb146ac7a?utm_source=openai))
- **Google DeepMind opens access to its AI “world model” project** — TechCrunch says Google is letting U.S. subscribers try Project Genie, a system for generating interactive game-like worlds from prompts or images, reflecting growing interest in “world models” as a frontier of AI research. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/01/29/i-built-marshmallow-castles-in-googles-new-ai-world-generator-project-genie/?utm_source=openai))
- **OpenAI releases a new child-safety blueprint** — TechCrunch’s AI headlines page lists a new OpenAI safety initiative aimed at addressing the rise in child sexual exploitation risks tied to AI systems. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/?utm_source=openai))
- **AWS defends backing both Anthropic and OpenAI** — TechCrunch highlights comments from AWS CEO Matt Garman explaining why Amazon sees strategic value in investing in multiple leading AI labs at once. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/?utm_source=openai))
- **Tubi launches a native app inside ChatGPT** — TechCrunch reports this as a notable sign of AI platform integration moving further into media and consumer apps. ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/category/artificial-intelligence/?utm_source=openai))

**Sources used:** AP and TechCrunch. I wasn’t able to retrieve fresh Reuters AI results in this search pass, so I’ve limited this roundup to outlets where current AI items were clearly available today or within the last few days. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/479eb3d0a50fe7237678a9bfb146ac7a?utm_source=openai))

If you want, I can also turn this into a **“5-minute AI news briefing”** or give you a **more enterprise/investor-focused roundup**.
